title: trifolium andinum var. navajoense
intro: Trifolium andinum var. navajoense, also known as the Navajo bean trefoil, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Trifolium andinum var. navajoense include its potential to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
